title: Password Hashing, Verification, and Upgrade · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt
summary: An account service stores unsalted SHA-256 passwords, benchmarks only the happy path, leaks user existence through timing, and cannot upgrade work factors. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the password hash not encrypt increment in versioned password credential verifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes.
Version derivation labels and parameters, preserve old read paths, prevent downgrade, and prove rotation rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt · Theory Crypto Go Password Hash No…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 4662 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit a versioned password verifier, bound KDF work, and select verify-then-rehash migration; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt contract for the supplied data.
